steps.title: Set up the quadratic equation
steps.content:
Area = length × width, so:
$$155 = (7w - 4) \cdot w$$
Expanding:
$$155 = 7w^2 - 4w$$
Rearranging to standard form:
$$7w^2 - 4w - 155 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Look for factors in the form $(7w + a)(w + b)$.
We need $ab = -155$ and $7b + a = -4$.
Since $155 = 5 \times 31$, try $a = 31$ and $b = -5$:
$(7w + 31)(w - 5) = 7w^2 - 35w + 31w - 155 = 7w^2 - 4w - 155$ ✓

steps.title: Solve for width
steps.content:
Setting each factor to zero:
$7w + 31 = 0 \Rightarrow w = -\frac{31}{7}$ (reject: width cannot be negative)
$w - 5 = 0 \Rightarrow w = 5$
The width of the rectangle is $5$ inches.
